mountains.desc: The Pamir Highway rises above the clouds. Eagle hunters, alpine lakes, and the roof of the world await.

cities.desc: Samarkand's blue domes glow at dawn. Bazaars pulse with spices, silk, and stories passed hand to hand.

partners.title: Our Partners
partners.subtitle: Local experts we trust on every Silk Road corridor
partners.intro: Every GREATSILKTRAILS package is delivered through vetted hotels, DMCs, and cultural institutions — not anonymous third parties. These are the teams that greet you at the airport, unlock closed monuments, and cook dinner in the desert.
partners.pageIntro: We work exclusively with partners who share our standards for safety, authenticity, and fair treatment of local communities. Below is the network behind your journey.

categoryDesc.transport: Air and road partners that connect your journey across borders.

pamiri-homestays.name: Pamiri Homestay Network
pamiri-homestays.desc: Community-owned lodges along the Wakhan Valley — authentic meals, apricot orchards, and the warmest welcomes on the Pamir Highway.

turkish-airlines.name: Turkish Airlines
turkish-airlines.desc: Preferred carrier for Istanbul connections to Tashkent, Bishkek, and Dushanbe with reliable schedules and generous baggage.

caravan-serai.name: Caravan Serai Group
caravan-serai.desc: Restored caravanserai properties in Bukhara and Khiva — sleeping where merchants rested for a thousand years.

nomad-trails.name: Nomad Trails Kyrgyzstan
nomad-trails.desc: Horse trekking, jailoo camps, and Issyk-Kul extensions — the Kyrgyz team behind our Heavenly Mountains itinerary.

uzbekistan-tourism.name: Uzbekistan Tourism Board
uzbekistan-tourism.desc: Official partner for heritage site access, festival calendars, and priority entry at major monuments during peak season.

blog.title: Road Journal
blog.subtitle: Stories, guides, and inspiration from the trail

samarkand-blue-domes.title: Why Samarkand's Blue Domes Will Change You
samarkand-blue-domes.excerpt: A dawn visit to Registan reveals colors that photographs can never capture.
body: Stand in Registan Square before the city wakes and you understand why merchants called Samarkand the jewel of the Silk Road. The turquoise tiles catch the first light in a way no camera can reproduce — a living blue that shifts from cobalt to aquamarine as the sun climbs.

Our Golden Caravan tour includes a private dawn visit with a local historian who reads the tilework like a manuscript. You learn which patterns are Timurid, which are Soviet restoration, and which stories the madrasahs still whisper to students at prayer time.
By the time tour buses arrive at nine, you have already walked the same stones as Ibn Battuta. That is the difference between visiting Samarkand and truly arriving there.

caravan-life-desert.title: A Night in the Karakum Desert
caravan-life-desert.excerpt: Sleeping under a billion stars, listening to the silence of the sands.
body: The Karakum does not reward hurry. On our Desert Echoes itinerary we leave the highway behind and set camp where caravans once rested between Merv and Khiva — no light pollution, no signal, only wind over dunes.

Dinner is cooked in a sand pit: lamb, onions, and rice sealed under embers the way nomads have done for centuries. Afterward, your guide points out constellations named by Persian astronomers long before Western star charts existed.
Sleep comes easily in a felt yurt. You wake to tea brewed on a dung fire and a horizon that has not changed in a thousand years.

pamir-highway-guide.title: The Ultimate Pamir Highway Guide
pamir-highway-guide.excerpt: Everything you need to know before tackling the world's most scenic road.
body: The Pamir Highway is not a road you conquer — it is one you respect. At 4,000+ metres, altitude affects everyone differently. We acclimatise in Osh and Dushanbe before the high passes, with medical kits and oxygen on every vehicle.

Border crossings between Tajikistan and Kyrgyzstan require paperwork we handle in advance. You need a valid passport, comfortable layers, and a willingness to spend six hours without Wi-Fi while the Wakhan Valley unfolds beside you.

The payoff is Afghanistan visible across the river, glaciers at arm's length, and homestays where Pamiri families serve apricot jam with every meal. Pack light, bring sunscreen, and trust your driver — ours have driven this route for decades.

silk-road-cuisine.title: Tasting the Silk Road: 10 Dishes You Must Try
silk-road-cuisine.excerpt: From plov in Tashkent to khinkali in Tbilisi — a culinary caravan.
body: Every GREATSILKTRAILS package treats food as culture, not fuel. In Uzbekistan that means plov cooked in massive kazans — rice, carrot, and lamb layered with cumin and barberry. In Kyrgyzstan it is beshbarmak eaten with your hands around a low table.

Do not skip lagman in Kashgar, manti steamed in Bishkek, or khachapuri on the Georgian extension. Our guides book family-run restaurants away from tourist traps and explain the trade-route origins of spices you thought were local.
Vegetarians and halal diets are accommodated with 48 hours' notice. Bring an appetite; portion sizes on the Silk Road are generous by design.

concierge.title: Silk Concierge
concierge.welcome: Hi! I'm your Silk Road travel assistant. Ask about visas, prices, what's included — or tap a quick topic below.
concierge.welcomeTour: You are viewing {tour}. Ask about this trip or reserve a 24h hold — payment is only requested after availability is confirmed.

answers.visa: We handle visa support and invitation letters for Uzbekistan, Turkmenistan, Tajikistan, and China. Send us your passport scan after booking — typical processing is 5–10 business days.
answers.price: All prices on the site are per person in USD, all-inclusive (hotels, guide, transfers, most meals). No hidden fees at checkout.
answers.cancel: More than 60 days before departure: full refund of amounts paid minus a $150 administrative fee per person. 30-60 days: 50% refund. Less than 30 days: no refund, but credit toward a future departure within 12 months (subject to availability).
answers.included: Every package includes boutique hotels, expert guide, airport transfers, entrance fees, breakfast + most dinners, and 24/7 on-trip support.
answers.solo: About 40% of our travelers come solo. We assign a roommate on request or arrange a single supplement — you're never alone in the group.
answers.payment: After we confirm availability, we request a deposit to secure your place. Balance is due before departure as stated in your confirmation. We do not take one-click online payment on the website.
answers.group: Small groups: Explorer tours up to 12 guests, Signature up to 8. Bespoke tours are fully private.
answers.when: Departures run year-round. Next dates are shown on each tour page — we can also arrange private departures for 4+ travelers.
answers.safety: We work only with licensed local partners. Comprehensive travel insurance is strongly recommended — we can suggest trusted providers.
answers.fallback: Great question! For details tailored to your dates, tap "Reserve this trip now" — a specialist replies within 24 hours. Or ask about visa, price, cancellation, or what's included.

concierge.tourPrice: {tour} is ${price} per person, all-inclusive. Tap Reserve to hold your spot for 24 hours.
concierge.tourWhen: Next departure for {tour}: {date}. Flexible? We'll find the best window for you.
concierge.tourSpots: Group size is limited on {tour}. Ask us for current availability on your preferred dates.

trekking.title: Trekking
trekking.subtitle: On-foot routes across the Pamir, Tien Shan, and Hindu Kush
trekking.body: We arrange guided trekking modules — from short day hikes to multi-day Pamir crossings — with local guides, permit support, and camp logistics. Trekking can be added to any Silk Trail package or booked as a standalone module.

servicesLogistics.title: Services & Logistics
servicesLogistics.subtitle: Visas, permits, transport, guides, and on-the-ground support across the Silk Road

visa-support.title: Visa Support & Invitation Letters
visa-support.desc: Invitation letters and visa guidance for Central Asia, Iran, and Pakistan itineraries.
visa-support.body: What we do: invitation letters where applicable, document checklists, embassy timing guidance, and coordination with your tour dates. What we cannot do: guarantee visa approval — embassies decide independently. GST does not promise visa outcomes. Process: 1) Share nationality and route 2) We confirm eligibility and documents 3) Invitation/letter preparation where available 4) You submit to the embassy or e-visa portal. Timing: typically 5-15+ business days depending on nationality and country. Start early for Iran, Turkmenistan, and multi-entry routes. Fees: service fees are quoted per case; government visa fees are separate. CTA: Plan Your Journey or contact a specialist with passport nationality and intended dates.

permits-gbao.title: Permits & Special Access (GBAO, etc.)
permits-gbao.desc: Pamir Highway (GBAO), border zones, and restricted-area permits for Tajikistan and beyond.
permits-gbao.body: Permit types we commonly arrange: GBAO (Pamir / Badakhshan), selected border-zone permissions, and special access tied to a confirmed itinerary. Documents: passport scan, photo, travel dates, route outline, and local partner requirements. Lead times: often 1-3+ weeks; border and military rules change — we only use current partner terms. Restrictions: some areas (including Sarez and certain border zones) may be closed or limited. We do not offer access that is not legally available at the time of travel. CTA: Plan Your Journey with intended dates and route; we confirm what can be arranged before payment.

transport-rental.title: Transport & Car Rental
transport-rental.desc: 4x4 fleets, intercity transfers, and private vehicles along overland corridors.
transport-rental.body: Vehicle categories: city sedans, intercity vans, and 4x4 expedition vehicles for high passes and remote corridors. Seats and luggage: sized to group count and road type — Pamir and Karakoram need robust 4x4 with space for gear. Driver included: most packages include driver; self-drive is rare and only where legally and practically suitable. Route suitability: Pamir Luxe Drive and similar overland modules use vehicles matched to altitude, season, and border crossings. CTA: request a transport plan with dates, route, and group size — or Plan Your Journey for a full private itinerary.

drivers-guides.title: Drivers & Guides
drivers-guides.desc: English-speaking drivers and specialist local guides for every corridor.
drivers-guides.body: Languages: English-speaking guides and drivers are standard on GST packages where stated; other languages on request when available — we do not promise languages we cannot staff. Guide vs driver-guide: city historians and mountain specialists differ from long-distance drivers; some days combine both. Route expertise: Pamir Highway veterans, oasis-city guides, and nomadic-region hosts in our partner network. Pricing: day rates or full-trip inclusion depending on package; private upgrades quoted on enquiry. CTA: Contact Specialist or Plan Your Journey with preferred language and route.

accommodation.title: Accommodation & Logistics
accommodation.desc: Hotels, guesthouses, yurts, and camp setups along your route.
accommodation.body: Levels we arrange: boutique/heritage hotels in cities; guesthouses and family homestays in valleys; yurts and simple camps on mountain or desert legs. What can be arranged: rooming (double/twin/single), meals coordination, remote camp logistics (water, fuel stops), and country-specific stay styles. Examples: Uzbekistan heritage hotels; Kyrgyz jailoo camps; Pamiri homestays; desert camps on Karakum modules. Scope: exact hotel names are confirmed with availability closer to departure; standards match the package tier you select. CTA: Plan Your Journey with comfort level (explorer / signature / private).

tailor-made.title: Tailor-Made Journeys
tailor-made.desc: Custom dates, private groups, and bespoke Silk Road routes.
tailor-made.body: Plan a Private Journey with GST: share countries, preferred dates, group size, comfort level, budget range, and interests (culture, trekking, photo, overland). Process: 1) Enquiry 2) Route outline and estimate 3) Availability check with partners 4) Confirmation terms 5) Deposit only after availability is confirmed. Ideal for: families, photographers, small private groups, and flexible dates outside fixed departures. CTA: use Plan Your Journey — no fixed tour needs to be selected first.

silkTrails.title: The Silk Road
silkTrails.subtitle: Xian-Istanbul corridor — nine flagship trails and side routes across living caravan countries
silkTrails.intro: GREATSILKTRAILS presents the historic Silk Road as a curated travel platform: fixed-price flagship trails, overland side routes (Pamir, Wakhan, Fann, Bartang, Sarez, Karakoram), and private journeys. Map points link to destinations and tours. Historical corridor notes are editorial and distinct from present-day operations.

sections.title: Operator and contact
paragraphs: GREATSILKTRAILS operates as a curated travel platform for Silk Road journeys. For privacy requests contact hello@greatsilktrails.com. Applicable operational partners and governing law for each booking are confirmed in your written confirmation before payment.
If you need the registered legal entity name and country of registration for a specific booking, request them with your enquiry — details are provided in the confirmation documents for that tour.

sections.title: Information We Collect
paragraphs: When you submit a booking inquiry, we collect your name, email address, optional phone number, selected tour, message content, and browser locale. We use this solely to respond to your request and prepare a travel proposal.
We do not sell personal data to third parties. Payment details, when collected, are processed by licensed payment providers and are not stored on our servers.

sections.title: How We Use Your Data
paragraphs: Inquiry data is stored securely and used to confirm availability, send itineraries, and communicate before, during, and after your trip. With your consent we may send occasional travel inspiration — you can unsubscribe at any time.
We may share necessary contact details with local ground partners (hotels, guides, transport) solely to deliver the services you booked.

sections.title: Your Rights
paragraphs: You may request access, correction, or deletion of your personal data by emailing hello@greatsilktrails.com. We respond within 30 days.
If you are in the EU or UK, you have additional rights under GDPR including data portability and the right to lodge a complaint with your supervisory authority.

sections.title: Legal entity and applicable law
paragraphs: Bookings are confirmed in writing after availability is checked. The contracting party, country of registration, and applicable law for your tour are stated in the booking confirmation and invoice before any deposit is due.
Submitting an enquiry or website form does not create a paid contract. Online display of prices is informational; final confirmation follows partner availability.

sections.title: Booking & Confirmation
paragraphs: Submitting an inquiry does not constitute a confirmed booking. A tour is confirmed only after we send written confirmation and receive the required deposit.
Prices quoted on the website are per person based on double occupancy unless stated otherwise. Single supplements, visa fees, and optional activities may apply.

sections.title: Traveler Responsibilities
paragraphs: You are responsible for valid passports (minimum 6 months validity), required visas, vaccinations, and travel insurance. We provide visa support letters where applicable but cannot guarantee embassy approval.
You must disclose health conditions that may affect participation, especially on high-altitude or physically demanding itineraries such as Pamir Odyssey.

sections.title: Itinerary Changes
paragraphs: We reserve the right to modify routes, accommodations, or activities due to weather, border closures, or force majeure. We will offer comparable alternatives of equal or greater value.
GREATSILKTRAILS acts as an intermediary between you and local service providers. Our liability is limited to the tour price paid, except where prohibited by applicable law.

sections.title: Standard Refund Schedule
paragraphs: More than 60 days before departure: full refund of amounts paid minus a $150 administrative fee per person. This is the same rule referenced across the website (Home, FAQ, Tour pages).
30–60 days before departure: 50% refund of the tour price.
Less than 30 days before departure: no refund, but credit toward a future departure within 12 months (subject to availability).

sections.title: Rebooking & Transfers
paragraphs: One free date change is permitted up to 45 days before departure, subject to availability and any fare differences.
If GREATSILKTRAILS cancels a departure, you receive a full refund or transfer to another date at no extra cost.

sections.title: Travel Insurance
paragraphs: We strongly recommend comprehensive travel insurance covering cancellation, medical evacuation, and trip interruption — especially for remote Silk Road segments.
Claims related to personal illness, visa denial after timely application, or failure to meet group departure points are not eligible for operator refunds beyond the schedule above.

china.name: China
china.desc: Eastern terminus of the Silk Road — where silk caravans began their westward journey.

kazakhstan.name: Kazakhstan
kazakhstan.desc: Northern Silk Road corridor through the Tian Shan — gateway between China and Central Asia.

kyrgyzstan.name: Kyrgyzstan
kyrgyzstan.desc: Mountain passes and nomadic routes linking the Fergana Valley to China.

uzbekistan.name: Uzbekistan
uzbekistan.desc: Heart of Transoxiana — UNESCO Silk Roads hub where east–west caravan routes converged.

turkmenistan.name: Turkmenistan
turkmenistan.desc: Zarafshan–Karakum corridor — a core UNESCO Silk Road segment across the Karakum.

tajikistan.name: Tajikistan
tajikistan.desc: Pamir branch of the Silk Road — high-altitude routes between Central Asia and China.

afghanistan.name: Afghanistan
afghanistan.desc: Southern Silk Road crossroads — the Hindu Kush linked Persia to the Indian subcontinent.

iran.name: Iran
iran.desc: Persian corridor — for centuries the main westward artery for silk, spices, and ideas.

pakistan.name: Pakistan
pakistan.desc: Karakoram branch — the southern route over the world's highest passes.

india.name: India
india.desc: Southern Silk Road terminus — spices, gems, and Buddhism flowed north along caravan trails.

georgia.name: Georgia
georgia.desc: Caucasus transit corridor — bridge between the Caspian and Black Sea trade networks.

armenia.name: Armenia
armenia.desc: Ancient crossroads of Persian and Anatolian caravan routes through the Caucasus.

azerbaijan.name: Azerbaijan
azerbaijan.desc: Caspian Sea corridor — linking Central Asian silk to Caucasus and beyond.

turkey.name: Turkey
turkey.desc: Western gateway of the Silk Road — Anatolian routes carried goods toward the Mediterranean.

corridors.east: Where the Silk Road began — Han dynasty routes into Central Asia.
corridors.tianshan: Northern route around the Taklamakan, through the Tian Shan mountains.
corridors.transoxiana: UNESCO Zarafshan–Karakum corridor — the richest segment of the Silk Road.
corridors.pamir: Pamir and Hindu Kush passes — among the most dramatic caravan routes on earth.
corridors.persia: Persian plateau — the great westward highway for silk and culture.
corridors.southAsia: Karakoram and Indus routes connecting Central Asia to the Indian subcontinent.
corridors.caucasus: Trans-Caucasian trade routes between the Caspian and Mediterranean worlds.
corridors.anatolia: Final overland stretch before goods reached Mediterranean ports.

heritage.intro: The Silk Road wasn't a single path — it was a network connecting empires, ideas, and treasures for over 2,000 years. Every GREATSILKTRAILS package follows in the footsteps of merchants, scholars, and explorers who shaped the world.

video.title: See the Silk Road in motion
video.subtitle: Desert caravans, golden dunes, ancient cities — experience what awaits on your journey.

silkRoad.title: 7,000 miles. One legendary route.
silkRoad.subtitle: From Xi'an to Istanbul — trace the path merchants traveled for millennia.
silkRoad.desc: Every GREATSILKTRAILS package follows authentic Silk Road corridors through living cities, desert crossings, and mountain passes.
silkRoad.mapSubtitle: From Chang'an, capital of Han China, to Constantinople — approx. 7,000 miles of caravan trails

xian.name: Xi'an
xian.tag: Eastern terminus — Terracotta Army & ancient capital

dunhuang.name: Dunhuang
dunhuang.tag: Mogao Caves & edge of the Gobi Desert

samarkand.name: Samarkand
samarkand.tag: Registan Square — jewel of the Silk Road

merv.name: Merv
merv.tag: Ancient oasis at the crossroads of empires

tehran.name: Tehran
tehran.tag: Persian heritage & gateway to the west
